name: Oatmeal Bread I
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the scalded milk, margarine, salt, and molasses in a bowl. Stir in the cold water until well combined and cooled to lukewarm.
2. In a warm, large bowl, pour the warm water and sprinkle in the yeast. Stir to dissolve.
3. Add the cooled mixture, oatmeal, and 4 cups of flour, mixing well. Continue adding flour and kneading on a lightly floured board until elastic (5-8 minutes).
4. Form the dough into a ball and place it in a greased bowl. Turn to grease all over. Cover with cloth and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. Punch down the dough and lightly knead for 1-2 minutes. Divide the dough into two equal parts. Shape each part into loaves and place in greased 9-by-5-by-3-inch loaf pans.
6. Cover and let rise until almost doubled in bulk.
7. Preheat the oven to 375F (190C) while the loaves are rising.
8. Bake the loaves for 40 minutes or until browned and hollow-sounding when tapped. Turn out onto a wire rack and cool upright before cutting. Makes 2 Loaves
